description: The 36 Boys were a gang of primarily Turkish immigrants based in Berlin-Kreuzberg, Germany, active from the late 1980s until the mid-1990s. Founded by Attila Murat Aydın, the gang engaged in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, arson, burglary, and vandalism. They marked their territory with graffiti and fought turf battles with Nazi and skinhead groups. After dissolution, some former members became social workers or pursued lawful careers.
